meta.subject: Spence paternal line — Hunter Spence's grandfather David Spence

confidence_tiers.CONFIRMED: Documentary primary source or multiple corroborating sources
confidence_tiers.POSSIBLE: Single source or plausible inference from pattern
confidence_tiers.UNVERIFIED: Family oral tradition or deduction only

surname_inheritance_question.question: Did Hunter inherit the Spence surname from his mother Rachel (who is David Spence's daughter) or from his biological father?
surname_inheritance_question.most_probable_scenario: Rachel (Shari + David's daughter) uses 'Spence' as her legal/active surname, and Hunter inherited it from her. This is Scenario A.
surname_inheritance_question.scenario_b: Hunter's biological father independently bears the Spence surname — possible but not supported by any accessible evidence.
surname_inheritance_question.note: Leroy Baity Mattingly's 2013 obituary names 'Sharyn Mattingly of Santa Monica, California' as surviving daughter — no reference to a married daughter with the surname Spence. This is consistent with Rachel using the maiden name Spence.
surname_inheritance_question.action_needed: Confirm with Hunter or Rachel directly

hunter_grandfather.name: David Spence
hunter_grandfather.birth_year_derivation: Approximately 80 years old as described in Shari's interview context c. 2025-2026
hunter_grandfather.birth_state_reasoning: Shari met David at Texas A&M University; San Antonio (Bexar County) family context; Deep Scottish/Irish settlement of Central Texas

marriage_to_shari.search_result: Texas Marriage Index 1966-1972 searched — no Spence + Mattingly match found. Pre-1966 records require county clerk access.
marriage_to_shari.probable_county: Bexar County (San Antonio) or Travis County (Austin/College Station area)

clan_spens_historical.classification: Lowland Scottish clan; also a sept of Clan MacDuff
clan_spens_historical.current_chief: Patrick Nathaniel George Spens, 4th Baron Spens
clan_spens_historical.motto: Si Deus Quis Contra (If God is for us, who is against us?)

earliest_documented_records.record: List of tenants/vassals of Walter fitz Alan, Steward of Scotland
Ragman Rolls — swore fealty to Edward I of England

earliest_documented_records.role: Bishop of Galloway; Bishop of Aberdeen; Lord Privy Seal of Scotland

earliest_documented_records.person: Patrick Spens (youngest son of John Spens of Lathallan)
earliest_documented_records.role: Sent by King James II of Scotland to Charles VII of France; founded Barony de Spens d'Estignols; family served as Garde du Corps du Roi until French Revolution 1789

orkney_spence_family.source: Orkney Family History Society — Kirkwall OPR film 0990503 (1652-1727)
orkney_spence_family.key_line: Spence of Scapa — documented from Harie Spence 1st of Scapa (pre-1657) through David Spence 5th of Scapa
orkney_spence_family.connection_to_basil_spence: Sir Basil Urwin Spence (1907-1976, architect of Coventry Cathedral) had 'Orcadian parents'. Father: Urwin Archibald Spence, assayer Royal Mint, Bombay.
orkney_spence_family.connection_to_hunter: POSSIBLE — same surname regional cluster; no direct genealogical link established

scottish_to_american_line.source: WikiTree Spence-4998, sourced from FamilySearch NC Estate Files and Ancestry.com

chain.significance: First documented Spence in colonial America from this Scottish line

scottish_to_american_line.note: This is one documented Scottish-American Spence line. Whether Hunter's grandfather David descends from it cannot be confirmed without his birth certificate.

kentucky_illinois_spence_line.source: WikiTree Spence-3467 (Williamson D. Spence), Spence-3471 (Malachi Spence)

chain.location: Born Pasquotank, North Carolina; died Livingston County, Illinois
Born Madison County, Kentucky; died Fairbury, Livingston County, Illinois
chain.married: Mary Darnall (4 Jun 1837, Indian Grove Township, IL)

kentucky_illinois_spence_line.relevance_to_navarro_county_spence: Both Williamson D. Spence (Illinois, 1814-1896) and David W. Spence (born Cass County, Illinois, 1840) are Spence families in Illinois from the same era, almost certainly from the same NC → Kentucky → Illinois migration wave. However, direct family connection not confirmed in this session.

irish_texas_spence_line.source: Smoky Mountain Ancestral Quest (smokykin.com), family group sheets F176045, F111328, F70913
irish_texas_spence_line.note: This is a separate Spence line from Ireland, NOT from Scotland, that reached San Antonio Texas by the late 19th century

chain.dates: born abt. 1767, Ireland; died abt. 1789, South Carolina
chain.confidence: PROBABLE (genealogy database, no primary source cited)

chain.married: Martha E. Perkins (11 Jan 1838, Smithville, Monroe County, Mississippi)

chain.location: Born Smithville, Monroe County, Mississippi; died San Antonio, Bexar County, Texas
chain.married: Mary Katherine 'Cassie' Ray (7 Jan 1902, Floresville, Wilson County, Texas)

chain.location: Born Wilson County, Texas; died San Antonio, Bexar County, Texas
chain.buried: Fort Sam Houston National Cemetery, San Antonio (Find a Grave #3051286)
chain.cause_of_death: Gunshot wound in head, self inflicted
chain.military_service: CONFIRMED (national cemetery burial eligibility requires military service)

navarro_county_texas_spence.source: txnavarr.genealogyvillage.com — transcribed from The Dawson Herald obituaries 1907-1945

head_of_family.birthplace: Cass County, Illinois (obit says 'Cask County' — almost certainly Cass County)
head_of_family.death_place: Two miles north of Dawson, Navarro County, Texas

navarro_county_texas_spence.relevance_to_hunter: NOT Hunter's direct grandfather — David W. Spence (1840-1907) is too early and his male line died out. Presented as evidence of the Illinois → Texas Spence migration corridor.

1840_illinois_census.record: A 'David Spence' appears in the 1840 Cass County, Illinois census as head of household
1840_illinois_census.interpretation: This is David W. Spence's father (David W. was born November 1840, the census was taken in August 1840 when he was not yet born or was an infant)

civil_war.finding: No Spence found in Navarro County Confederate rosters (Moss's Company, Dresden's Texas Cavalry, Winkler's Company, Martin's Company). David W. Spence (born 1840, in Texas 1850-1907) was of service age 1861-1865 but no record found.
civil_war.confidence: UNVERIFIED (absence of evidence, not evidence of absence)

wwi.recommended_search: Fold3.com WWI draft registration cards — search 'Spence' in Navarro County or Bexar County Texas

wwii.finding: Benjamin 'Ben' Spence (1905-1951, Wilson County TX) confirmed military service — buried Fort Sam Houston National Cemetery
wwii.source: Smoky Mountain Ancestral Quest (smokykin.com), Find a Grave #3051286

notable_spences_new.name: Henry de Spens
notable_spences_new.role: Feudal baron; swore fealty to Edward I of England on Ragman Rolls 1296
notable_spences_new.significance: First individually named Spens in primary records

notable_spences_new.name: Bishop Thomas Spens
notable_spences_new.role: Bishop of Galloway; Bishop of Aberdeen; Lord Privy Seal of Scotland

notable_spences_new.name: Patrick Spens
notable_spences_new.role: Founded French aristocratic line Barony de Spens d'Estignols; family served as Garde du Corps du Roi to French kings until 1789

notable_spences_new.name: David Spence of Dysart
notable_spences_new.significance: Likely ancestor of colonial American Spence families in Virginia/Maryland/NC

recommended_searches.action: Request Texas birth certificate for David Spence born ~1944-1948
recommended_searches.notes: Hunter qualifies as grandson. Cost approx. $22. This would confirm parents' names.
recommended_searches.expected_yield: Birth date, exact birthplace, parents' full names

recommended_searches.action: Search Texas Marriage Index pre-1966 for Spence + Mattingly in Bexar or Travis County
recommended_searches.notes: The 1966-1972 index search was completed with no match found. Marriage may be 1963-1965.

recommended_searches.action: Confirm Rachel Spence's legal name — is 'Spence' her active surname?
recommended_searches.notes: This resolves the surname inheritance question definitively. Ask Hunter.

recommended_searches.action: 1940 US Census search for Spence families in Bexar County and Travis County Texas
recommended_searches.notes: David's father (born ~1915-1925) would appear here. Free on FamilySearch.

recommended_searches.action: ScotlandsPeople index search — Dysart, Fife: David Spence b. 1639
recommended_searches.notes: Verify WikiTree Spence-4998 claim. Costs ~£7 in credits.

confidence_summary.surname_scottish_origin: CONFIRMED (Clan Spens of Fife, documented from 1161)
confidence_summary.scottish_virginia_colonial_connection: PROBABLE (WikiTree Spence-4998, David Spence of Dysart 1639)
confidence_summary.military_service_confirmed: CONFIRMED for Benjamin Ben Spence (WWII, Fort Sam Houston)
